channel.title: RetroMac
channel.description: Most recent changes with links to updates.

item.title: Version 1.9.7
item.description:
RetroMac 1.9.7 — two new Mac themes, a retro TV & matching system looks

  • New theme — Mac OS 6 classic: a black-and-white System 6 desktop with 1-bit icons, selectable 8×8 desktop patterns, Chicago/Geneva fonts, a Control Strip that can switch to a 2D Platinum dock, and monochrome app icons.
  • Mac OS 9 merged + Platinum widgets: the old “Mac OS 9.2” is now a Platinum-dock option inside Mac OS 9 Classic; every Mac OS 9/6 desktop widget got a pixel-faithful Platinum title bar with working close/collapse/zoom.
  • TV Tube mode: one click turns your streams into a floating retro TV — a free-standing TV in a window, or fullscreen on an external display with a downloadable bezel and the Joel GDV-NTSC shader; right-click to change channel, shader, bezel or TV set.
  • Themes match your Mac: optional per-theme macOS appearance + accent (Graphite for Mac OS …) and a matching Terminal profile (DOS green, BeOS, Classic Mac); your originals are remembered and restored. A crown marks the special themes.
  • Real screensavers: install Pipes, FlowerBox, Flying Toasters and Flurry as genuine macOS .saver modules.
  • More: unified Mac dock icon sizes (desktop icons follow the size slider), Snow Leopard / Mountain Lion shown as “Mac OS …”, sturdier crash recovery, and many smaller fixes.

item.title: Version 1.9.6
item.description:
RetroMac 1.9.6 — a meaner DOOM dock, a 60% smaller download & fixes

  • DOOM theme, real firefights: authentic muzzle-flash animations for every weapon, a Plasma bolt stream, rocket & new BFG explosions, and charging Lost Souls that frag the Slayer if he shoots too late — he now only dies to demons or your mouse, with the full 6-frame fall.
  • 60% smaller download: an asset diet shrank the app from ~151 MB to ~61 MB — same themes, faster downloads and updates.
  • Skippable boot videos: a click or key now skips the Windows XP / 98 / Mac boot sequences straight to the desktop.
  • External displays: the retro menu-bar Apple logo sits correctly on extended monitors.
  • Magnification polish: the running-app dot stays centred under its icon on the Mac OS X / Snow Leopard docks; Snow Leopard joins the special themes.
  • Respects your settings: Retro Mode restores only what it hid (your own Dock auto-hide / hidden desktop icons stay), and the CRT Lite overlay sticks to one window per app.

item.title: Version 1.9.5
item.description:
RetroMac 1.9.5 — a new DOOM theme, a 24-hour clock & lots of fixes

  • New theme — Maiks Favourite II (DOOM): a pixel Doom Slayer patrols the dock (runs, switches weapons, fires, gets fragged) and blasts a Lost Soul that flies in. Hover him to frag him, click the DOOM logo tile to launch DOOM, plus a new “Apple Hell” menu-bar logo.
  • 24-hour clock: new option for the dock and deskbar clocks, plus a digital readout under the analog Clock widget that follows it.
  • Retro menu-bar Apple: Rainbow, Aqua, Aqua Classic or the new Apple Hell logo — cycle it from the flyout, with per-theme defaults.
  • Reliability: your real Dock reliably returns after turning a theme off and never peeks out beside the retro dock; Mac OS X / Snow Leopard magnification no longer reacts outside the dock; the floating launcher stays clickable beside a dock.
  • More: corrected rocket / death sprites, CPU monitor wording fix, and a clearer privacy & offline note in About.

item.title: Version 1.9.2
item.description:
RetroMac 1.9.2 — a retro menu-bar Apple, iPhone camera & a smoother Dock

  • Retro menu-bar Apple: cover the system Apple with the classic Rainbow, Aqua or Aqua Classic logo — cycle it from the flyout. Snow Leopard and Mac OS 9 pick fitting defaults.
  • iPhone as a camera: use your iPhone (Continuity Camera) or any webcam as the CRT-shaded virtual camera — the source list updates live as devices connect.
  • Smoother Dock + Snow Leopard 3D: fluid, real-Dock-style magnification, a corrected Snow Leopard 3D glass shelf, and no more background windows jumping to the front on a theme switch.
  • “Dock only” & calmer themes: Dock only restyles just the dock; the retro TV window and full-screen shader behave reliably, even across Spaces.
  • Dock Mode & quick launcher: show RetroMac in the Dock plus a floating button; one click switches themes and toggles the shader or camera.
  • More: Windows 98/XP taskbar, shaders on every display, GDV-NTSC “Retro Crisis” shaders, softer phosphor masks, paste-able license field, and many fixes.

item.title: Version 1.9.1.1
item.description:
RetroMac 1.9.1.1 — iPhone camera, a true “Dock only” & lots of fixes

  • iPhone as a camera source: pick your iPhone (Continuity Camera) or any webcam in Settings ▸ Camera & Streaming — the list updates live as devices connect, and the CRT shader runs on the feed.
  • “Dock only” really means dock only: switching a theme with Dock only on no longer touches your wallpaper, desktop icons, widgets or boot splash, and won’t start a full-screen shader.
  • Multi-monitor Lite shaders: the lightweight (Lite) presets now appear on the display you select, not just the main one.
  • TV windows behave: the retro TV window no longer borrows the last used theme’s menu bar / chrome when no theme is active.
  • Tidier quick-access flyout: the shader toggle and preset dropdown now stay correct after switching presets; active theme highlighted correctly; Settings/Quit are clean icons; floating launcher on by default.
  • Dock fixes: clicking a Dock tile reliably brings a running app’s window to the front; correct flyout & Dock-Mode icons across themes; desktop-icon visibility now respects other apps.

item.title: Version 1.9.0
item.description:
RetroMac 1.9 — Dock launcher, taskbar tabs & shaders on every screen

  • Dock Mode & quick launcher: optionally show RetroMac in the Dock; click its icon for a slim launcher to switch themes and toggle the shader or virtual camera. Turn it on in Settings ▸ Dock.
  • Authentic Windows taskbar: Windows 98 and XP now show one elongated taskbar button per open window — click to minimize or restore, just like the real thing.
  • Shaders on all your displays: the CRT shader now works on secondary monitors — “All Displays” lights up every screen, not just the main one.
  • New Retro Crisis shaders + fixes: two GDV-NTSC “Retro Crisis” looks (Composite & RGB), and the classic 98.js desktop apps (Notepad, Paint, Minesweeper…) work again.
  • Quick fix: the Settings, Quit and Retro Mode icons in the menu could go missing on a dark desktop — they’re back and reliable.

item.title: Version 1.8.2
item.description:
RetroMac 1.8.2

  • Webcam lower-third fixes: the on-screen name bar no longer flickers, the Late Night (Gold) / Newsroom style now follows your setting, and long names fit the bar.
  • Built-in diagnostics: Settings ▸ About ▸ Diagnostics captures a log you can copy or save — making display, camera and dock issues much easier to track down.
  • Plus stability hardening: the system Dock reliably comes back after quitting, and assorted dock/window fixes.

item.title: Version 1.8.1
item.description:
RetroMac 1.8 — new Special Themes, boot screens & screensavers

  • New Special Themes: Windows 98 (with classic games and sheep.exe!), Windows XP, and Mac OS X with its glossy Aqua look — plus classic apps like Internet Explorer, Notepad and Paint.
  • Boot screens: authentic startup videos when you switch to Windows XP, Windows 98 or Mac OS 9 — click to skip to the desktop.
  • Classic screensavers: a per-theme saver (3D Pipes, FlowerBox, Flying Toasters, Flurry) — pick yours in Settings ▸ Screensaver.
  • Smarter minimize into the themed dock, new CPU Monitor & Desktop Clock widgets, plus widget, dock and stability fixes throughout.

item.title: Version 1.8.0
item.description:
RetroMac 1.8 — three new Special Themes, and even more retro vibes

  • New Special Themes — true to the originals: Windows 98 (with classic games and sheep.exe!), Windows XP, and Mac OS X with its glossy Aqua look.
  • Surf the past: under Windows XP and Windows 98 you can now browse the web with Internet Explorer — just like back in the day.
  • Smarter minimize: windows now minimize into the right dock — the themed dock instead of the system Dock.
  • New widgets & lots of polish: fresh widgets like the CPU Monitor and Desktop Clock, plus stability improvements and a ton of detail work.

item.title: Version 1.7.3
item.description:
RetroMac 1.7.3

  • Clock widget: now remembers its last position and no longer jumps on screen when opened.
  • BeOS Classic: drag the clock by its yellow tab again.
  • CPU widget: opens directly at its saved spot — no more visible jump.
  • Dock right-click now offers “RetroMac Settings” and “Quit RetroMac”.

item.title: Version 1.7.2
item.description:
RetroMac 1.7.2

  • Clock widget: a themed analog clock (BeOS, Mac OS 9, Windows XP, Maiks Favourite) — open it from the deskbar/taskbar clock or a desktop icon.
  • Maiks Favourite: pixel-art macOS window chrome for the CPU widget, App Folder, Music (TV streams) folder and the Downloads dock fan.
  • Menu polish: themes and shaders sorted alphabetically; a crown marks the special themes; the permission-free “Lite” shaders sit right under Free.
  • Stronger LCD Lite shader, clean start (nothing auto-activated), and numerous fixes.

item.title: Version 1.7.0
item.description:
RetroMac 1.7 — The big themes overhaul

  • Themes Overhaul — BeOS, Windows XP & Mac OS 9: a massive rework, as close to the originals as possible — including a native CPU widget and authentically styled windows in each theme.
  • New theme “Maiks Favourite”: an animated Pac-Man dock and live previews of your running apps.
  • Webcam support stabilized: more reliable virtual-camera toggling and retro effects.
  • Stability & fixes: numerous bug fixes and general stability improvements.

item.title: Version 1.3
item.description:
RetroMac 1.3

  • Dock auto-hide: reclaim screen space on 1080p monitors — dock slides in on mouse proximity
  • Freedoom: free Doom experience with native CRT shader (auto-download)
  • Quake 1 & 2 via RetroArch with CRT shader and WASD controls
  • Native PK3 shaders for Duke Nukem 3D and Shadow Warrior (zero input lag)
  • Removed all overlay-based game shaders — native engine shaders only

item.title: Version 1.1
item.description:
RetroMac 1.1

  • Automatic updates via Sparkle
  • Fixed theme switching crash (BeOS to Snow Leopard)
  • Dynamic trash icon (empty/full state)
  • Improved Screen Recording permission onboarding
  • User-friendly error messages for missing permissions
